verdicts.note: The record claims exists='yes' and links to https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid-cli, but that URL is the Mermaid CLI GitHub repo itself—not an MCP server. The evidence from https://mermaid.ai/docs/ai/mcp-server describes a *separate* MCP server (the 'Mermaid MCP Server' hosted at https://mcp.mermaid.ai) that wraps Mermaid functionality, not one dedicated to mermaid-cli. The correct setting is exists='no' or 'unknown' because there is no evidence of a dedicated MCP server *for the Mermaid CLI tool itself*.
The provided URL points to the Mermaid CLI GitHub repository, not to an MCP server repository or service endpoint. This must be removed.

The evidence shows Mermaid CLI is an open-source, local-only command-line tool with no hosted API. It requires local installation and runs as a Node.js library or CLI. There is no hosted/public API endpoint for remote calls. Per the skeptical standard, a tool that cannot be called over a hosted API is NOT 'buildable-now'—it should be 'blocked' or 'buildable-with-caveats' with a clear blocker statement.

The record states null, but the evidence clearly shows that Mermaid CLI is a local-only tool with no hosted service or public API. The main blocker is: 'No hosted API; Mermaid CLI is a local command-line tool that must be installed and run on the user's machine.'
Evidence confirms no authentication is required for the local Mermaid CLI tool. This field is accurate.
Evidence confirms the tool is freely available on GitHub and npm without registration or API keys. 'self-serve-free' is accurate.
The notes accurately describe the installation and availability via npm and GitHub.
Evidence confirms the tool provides a Node.js API and CLI; no REST or GraphQL API exists. 'SDK-only' is appropriate.
'narrow' is appropriate given the tool is focused solely on rendering Mermaid diagrams locally.
The notes accurately describe the Node.js API and CLI functionality, and note the lack of REST/GraphQL APIs.
The description is accurate: it is indeed an open-source CLI tool and Node.js API for rendering Mermaid diagrams locally without authentication.
The evidence confirms this is a tool for rendering diagrams, fitting 'AI, Research and Media-native'.
All evidence URLs are valid and support the various claims in the record.
0.95 is reasonable given the clear evidence about the tool's nature, though some fields require correction.

The flags 'open-source', 'local-tool', 'no-cloud-service', 'cli-and-sdk' are all accurate. However, 'existing-mcp-servers' is misleading—the evidence shows an MCP server for Mermaid (the library), not for Mermaid CLI specifically.

revised.name: Mermaid CLI
revised.one_liner: Open-source CLI tool and Node.js API for rendering Mermaid diagrams locally without authentication

auth_methods.notes: No authentication required. Mermaid CLI is a local rendering tool that runs as a command-line utility or Node.js library without any API authentication.

revised.self_serve_notes: Mermaid CLI is a fully open-source tool available on GitHub and npm. Users can install it globally via npm or use it programmatically without any registration, API keys, or account creation. Simply run 'npm install -g @mermaid-js/mermaid-cli' to get started.

api_surface.notes: Provides a Node.js API via the 'run' function from '@mermaid-js/mermaid-cli' package and a CLI tool (mmdc). The API is focused specifically on rendering Mermaid diagrams to SVG, PNG, or PDF formats. No REST or GraphQL API exists for the CLI itself, though third-party services like Beauty Diagram offer HTTP APIs for Mermaid rendering.

revised.main_blocker: No hosted API; Mermaid CLI is a local command-line tool that must be installed and run on the user's machine. There is no public/hosted service endpoint to call remotely.

evidence.supports: Official GitHub repository for mermaid-cli with 4.8k stars, confirming it's an open-source tool for rendering Mermaid diagrams
Installation via npm without authentication, usage via CLI (mmdc command) or Node.js API
Package available on npm as '@mermaid-js/mermaid-cli', demonstrates self-serve free installation
Node.js programmatic API usage with 'run' function for rendering diagrams locally
Mermaid.js library is open-source and free to use without API or subscription
A separate MCP server exists for Mermaid (the library), hosted at https://mcp.mermaid.ai, but this is not an MCP server dedicated to the Mermaid CLI tool itself.
